recovery_profile.notes: Sleep quantity is the dominant constraint; outdoor minutes are well below the 7h/week resilience threshold.

fastest_wins: Anchor a 30-minute outdoor walk before 10am, four days a week — directly raises recovery capacity by 6–9 points.
Cap caffeine at three cups by 2pm to protect sleep depth.
Block one no-meeting morning per week for focused work to lower pressure_frequency.

seven_day_plan.monday: Outdoor walk 30 min · 2L water · last coffee by 2pm
seven_day_plan.saturday: 60 min outdoor + family time · alcohol cap 2 units
seven_day_plan.sunday: Active recovery walk · weekly review · phone off after 9pm

data.personal_profile_snapshot: 47-year-old founder, high responsibility, chronic mild sleep debt, undertrained outdoor exposure, strong social support. Burnout risk is elevated but reversible with sleep + outdoor + caffeine timing levers.
data.closing_recommendation: Your recovery and resilience score suggests several opportunities to improve stress adaptation, energy management, and long-term performance. This assessment is educational only. If symptoms persist or are affecting your quality of life, consider a comprehensive executive wellness evaluation through WellneSpirit.
data.disclaimer: This assessment is educational and informational only. It is not a medical diagnosis, psychological diagnosis, or burnout diagnosis. It should not replace consultation with a qualified healthcare professional.
